diagnosed: The chess fetcher's config (scripts/config/sports-config.js) still declared a 'curated' source pointing at scripts/config/chess-tournaments.json and scripts/config/norwegian-chess-players.json — both removed in the v2 rebuild (chess is an API-less sport the research agent maintains per CLAUDE.md). fetchCuratedTournaments therefore threw ENOENT for both files on EVERY static-pipeline run ('Failed to load .../chess-tournaments.json: ENOENT ...' x2). It degraded gracefully (loadJsonFile falls back to []) and Lichess still returned 64 active broadcasts, so nothing failed — but the recurring error noise masks real fetch failures and the dead source references files that will never exist.
fixed: Removed the dead 'curated' source from the chess config in scripts/config/sports-config.js, leaving Lichess as the only live source (plus the research agent for off-API events, per v2 design). Added a comment explaining why, and updated the source label to 'Lichess + AI research'.
notes: Non-protected path (scripts/config/sports-config.js, not one of the three protected paths), so the workflow may auto-merge once tests pass.

Proof: re-ran node scripts/fetch/index.js — the two chess ENOENT lines are gone, Lichess still reports '64 active, 0 upcoming'. Full npm test green (173 passing). node scripts/validate-events.js: 44 events, 0 errors. Regenerated docs/data/*.json were reverted (pipeline-owned content, not part of this fix).
